draft.introduction: John 3:16 is the most famous verse in Christianity. Spoken to Nicodemus, it compresses the entire Gospel into a single sentence: God loved the world, gave his Son, and offers eternal life to those who believe.

translation.text: For God so loved the world, that he gave his only-begotten Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish but have eternal life. For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but in order that the world might be saved through him.
translation.original_text: οὕτως γὰρ ἠγάπησεν ὁ θεὸς τὸν κόσμον

translation.text: And this is the judgment: the light has come into the world, and people loved the darkness rather than the light because their works were evil. But whoever does what is true comes to the light.
translation.original_text: αὕτη δέ ἐστιν ἡ κρίσις ὅτι τὸ φῶς ἐλήλυθεν εἰς τὸν κόσμον

translator_notes.note: The word οὕτως is typically rendered “so” (as in “so much”), but it more precisely means “in this way”—pointing to the manner of God’s love (giving the Son) rather than its intensity. The verse is not about human feeling for God but about God’s costly action toward the world.
